Milestone M1: grounded ladder as data-coherence test

Closed by node 51; superseded as a forward path by the SGM redirection (node 53). Run the grounded world ladder and read whether program-authored coherence lifts in-context binding clear of chance at fixed model size, with the mini full rung giving a direct mini-on-ungrounded versus mini-on-grounded comparison.

The next full world.yaml run generates grounded data (node 46) across the pico-to-full ladder (node 43), with run-id isolation so it does not disturb existing runs. Because the full rung is already the mini preset, it is a clean same-size comparison against the mini-on-ungrounded result (node 47). Opening gate: in-context binding exact-match (node 33) rising clearly above the guessing floor, a target near a quarter overall on the full rung and on both stages, with comparison and retrieval sub-scores read separately since retrieval clearing zero is the stronger signal. Secondary: grammar and coherence at least matching the mini-on-ungrounded baseline (grounding must not cost fluency) and the followed score rising, since grounded pairs state the facts in the user turn.

This milestone tests the central bet behind grounded generation: that a small model's failure to bind referents was a property of loose training data, not only of scale. The instrument is the binding probe (node 33), chosen over judged axes because it isolates the targeted capability with exact match and no judge noise, and over the accuracy probe (node 28) because parametric recall is not what the project trains. The gate mirrors node 45: binding well clear of the floor, sustained across rungs rather than at one scale. The floor is not uniform: two-way comparison questions sit near one half if the model merely echoes either name, while retrieval questions sit near zero, so the sub-scores are tracked apart and a rise in retrieval is weighted most. A diagnostic comes free from the ladder: the pico, nano, micro, and full rungs yield a binding-versus-data-fraction curve on identical-quality nested corpora, which directly feeds the scale-axis decision in M2 (node 49). Failure modes to watch: binding stays at floor (coherence alone is insufficient and the world-fragment verbalization or pair format needs work), or fluency drops (the grounding constrains the writer too tightly). The run is gated to not proceed to graph experiments (node 50) until this binding gate opens. Resolved (node 51): the grounded ladder ran and the gate opened at 0.78 versus the 0.25 bar, with fluency held (coherence 7 at full). Secondary criteria were partially misread at first because the evaluation still asked ungrounded real-world questions; the matched evaluation (node 52) fixes the instrument. The rung curve answered the M2 axis question with a plateau from micro up, redirecting M2 toward richer task families rather than either scale axis. The milestone is closed: its positive result (grounding opened the binding gate) and its negative lesson (a fully grounded corpus destroys general language) both feed the SGM redirection (node 53), and the grounded ladder corpora are retired to baselines.
